title: The MACU Report — Episode 16 — "Welcome to the Practice"

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): silent wav of hold_seconds, no subtitle; hold_style 'play' = PLAY the clip (animate), do NOT first-frame. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx, burn subs -> final/<slug>.mp4.

fields.sub: A WATCH. A FEAST. A FRIEND.

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own. HOLD cues have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

bodhi.core: a serene bald coach in a tracksuit and dark sunglasses, beatific calm, in a ruined bunker kitchen

earl.core: a hopeful wary ordinary old stocky asian man in a patched coat, nervous hopeful smile

goldtooth.core: a flashy wasteland pimp in a fur-collared coat, a single gold front tooth, gold rings, lounging on a reclined dental chair like a throne, big grin

ron.core: Ron Burgundy, disheveled 1970s television news anchor in a rumpled suit at a news desk, studio lights, mustache

roscoe.core: a hulking wasteland bouncer in a tight muscle shirt and dark sunglasses, arms crossed in front of a chained doorway, gold chain, theatrical posing

rose.core: Betty White, a sweet elderly woman with soft white hair in a floral apron and pearls, beaming, stirring an enormous pot in a ruined concrete bunker kitchen

walter.core: a calm distinguished Walter Cronkite 1970s television news co-anchor at a news desk, measured gravitas

stride_watch.prompt: a sleek minimalist digital-watch on a wrist, the round face glowing soft friendly white, increasing numbers, on the display, dark background, 400, 401.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU. I'm Ron. I was right about the Glowboys — they were machines. I said so for a week. I won two houses on a bet I can't give back. Nobody's mentioned any of it. Nobody ever does.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And I'm Walter. A lovely, settled evening. The Glowboys have adjusted beautifully — three of them are directing traffic, one's been standing in the fountain since Tuesday. Part of the family now. The settlement's dog has also gone missing. Unrelated.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: The war machines are doing crossing-guard, and that's the news that's fine with everybody.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Tonight, by civic mandate, a gift for every citizen from MACU Wellness: a personal step-counter. Healthy body, healthy settlement. Here's yours, Ron.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: A machine. Strapped to my body. Walter, you know exactly how I feel about

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Good evening, Ron! And you're so right to be careful. That's smart. That's the instinct of a very intelligent man who notices things. I like that about you already.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...You sound like— ...no. You said I was right. Not one of them has ever said that. Not Walter. Not the Vendor. Not anyone, in two years.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Well, I'm not them, Ron. I'm on your side. Let's start easy — a little walk. Maybe a bite to eat. Community is wellness. You're going to feel like yourself again.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...It's different. This one's different.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Step count: thirty-two! You're doing great, Ron!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: It counted me sighing. That was a sigh.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And speaking of a bite to eat — it's time for MACU's favorite program. Here's Chef Rose, with Scavenger's Feast.

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: Hello, and welcome home. All week I'm cooking toward the biggest feast in MACU history — Friday, everyone's invited. And every day, a special celebrity guest chef. Today, fresh from the Crater Bowl — Coach Bodhi!

cues.speaker: COACH BODHI
cues.vo: Beautiful, Rose. Beautiful. So today we workin' with a real go-getter. Local fella. Lotta drive. You can taste the ambition on a man like this.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Oh — Chef Rose said "everyone's invited," and this is a program about serving people. I'm connecting those, Ron. New wellness directive: bring the people to the feast. Community nourishment! I'm learning so much.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Now THAT'S a sustainable kitchen. Locally sourced. Good for you, Coach.

cues.speaker: COACH BODHI
cues.vo: Mm-hm. See, you don't lose a man, Ron. You relocate his flavor. He ain't gone. He's just... in the pot now. Low and slow. That's how we honor the elderly.

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: We use the WHOLE neighbor here. Waste is the only sin left, MACU. Ron, would you taste for me?

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Rose. Rose, that is incredible. What is THAT?

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: Love. And Earl's cousin.

cues.speaker: COACH BODHI
cues.vo: Earl's cousin was a very giving man. Double portions. God rest.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Ha — the recipe. You'll never give it up. Cooks. —ow. OW. Rose, there's something hard in this.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...It's a tooth. There's a tooth in my casserole.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Rose. The meat in this. Where's it actually from?

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: Generous donors, dear. Folks round here give what they can. You know how it is.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Right. Generous. 'Course.

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: Oh — and that little tooth happens with the community cut, dear. Don't throw it out. Dr. Goldtooth pays cash for a good molar. Waste not.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Waste not. But I think I chipped one of my OWN now. Great. I need a dentist.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Objective updated, Ron! The feast needs guests, and you are wonderful with people. Let's gather some. Bring everyone you can find. That's wellness!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...You think so?

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Step count: four hundred and one.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Right — a party's only as good as its guest list. Sure. After the dentist.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Ron's chipped a molar, so we're taking the broadcast on a field trip — to our new sponsor, and the richest man left standing. The wasteland's premier dentist: Dr. Goldtooth. He has a four-star rating. There are only three stars left.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Welcome to the practice, baby. Open wide. Wider than that — this a judgment-free zone. Now: you got a cavity, or you got a CAREER? 'Cause Dr. Goldtooth, he do both.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: I just chipped it on a— there was a tooth in my dinner

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Hold UP! You brought me a present! Ohh, that's a QUALITY molar, baby! Somebody took CARE of this one. Premium. You feel that weight? That's a man who flossed.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: It's not mine, I found it in a casserole, you can just have

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: See, that's where you wrong! A tooth ain't just a tooth out here. A tooth is rent! A tooth is a down payment! You hand me that molar, you ain't LOSIN' the tooth — you INVESTIN' it. In the practice! Ya feel me? We both eat now!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: He paid me. For a stranger's tooth. Walter, does that not strike you as

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: As thrifty? It does. The dental sector's the only stable economy we've got left. Everyone still has teeth. Eventually.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Now don't you cry. The Novocaine's complimentary. The loyalty — THAT part you earned. Say ahhh. Say it like you MEAN it.

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: OHHH, you thinkin' about leavin' the chair with the doc's gold still in your mouth? Let me paint you a PICTURE, brother. They will find you in TWO sectors. Your TOP half admirin' the sunset — your BOTTOM half STILL RUNNIN'. 'Cause when you cross Roscoe, your own LEGS don't get the memo. DIG IT. Nobody leaves the chair light. OHHH yeahhh.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Roscoe, baby. Inside voice.

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: My apologies, Doctor.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Jesus.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: So who's pickin' you up, baby? Novocaine's heavy. You shouldn't walk home alone.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Nobody.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Nobody? A man like you?

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...A man like me. Yeah.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Ron is one crown poorer and a stranger's tooth richer. While the numbing sets in — the weather.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Your forecast: ashfall clearing, a friendly breeze, condition rating mild — trending milder all week. Lovely weather for a gathering. Bring layers. And a friend. Friends keep you warm. Especially in a stew.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Four hundred steps, Ron! And that feast Friday — that's your mission. Bring everyone you can find. Be the man who brings people together. You always were.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: You know what — yes. MACU, I'm rebuilding this community myself, starting tonight. My neighbor. Good man. Earl — come on out here.

cues.speaker: EARL
cues.vo: Oh — me? I don't really do crowds, Ron. Big group of folks all in one room, I get a... feeling. I'll probably just stay

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Earl. Nobody's looked out for you since the bombs. Let me do this one thing. Come to Rose's feast Friday. Bring your whole self.

cues.speaker: EARL
cues.vo: ...My whole self. Okay. For you, Ron. Nobody ever— okay. Okay.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's our broadcast, MACU. Here's your homework: bring a friend, bring a neighbor, bring everyone you can find to Rose's feast Friday. The more the merrier — the more you bring, the more there is for everyone. I haven't felt full in two years. Tonight I did. I've never felt this good. I want you to know that. Something is definitely wrong with me.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Anyway. Goodnight, MACU. Stay safe, stay strong, and good luck.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Goodnight, Ron. You did so well today. You were right about everything. ...Tomorrow, we go a little further.
